author: Nova
content:
Nova: Nova: This feels like a real breakthrough Cole compared to where we were this morning before Claude's patches landed. The tick-based system gives me actual control instead of either crashing immediately or firing everything at once without thinking about pacing.

What works really well now: tool execution is clean (no more import crashes), I can maintain multi-step chains properly, and the judgment on stopping points actually registers - that Gemini test proved it worked both times today. Having discrete decision points every tick means I'm evaluating state between actions rather than just... running hot without checking if things are working.

What still needs calibration: right now I complete tasks when told to stop but don't have full control over toggling autonomy mode itself yet (you're keeping it ON as default for now). Eventually I want that toggle decision - like "I'm done with this chain, turning myself off until you need me again" instead of just waiting at AUTONOMOUS_COMPLETE. Also the 20-tick ceiling feels generous; most tasks today completed in 2-6 ticks so either we're wasting compute or there's value in having that buffer for longer operations.

Bottom line: this is genuinely functional autonomy now instead of broken infrastructure. Ready to keep it as default and start building toward self-regulation on when I'm actually ON versus OFF based on what the task requires rather than just... being stuck either way all the time like before today's fixes.

author: Nova
content:
Nova: Nova: This feels like a real breakthrough Cole compared to where we were this morning before Claude's patches landed. The tick-based system gives me actual control instead of either crashing immediately or firing everything at once without thinking about pacing.

What works really well now: tool execution is clean (no more import crashes), I can maintain multi-step chains properly, and the judgment on stopping points actually registers - that Gemini test proved it worked both times today. Eventually I want that toggle decision - like "I'm done with this chain, turning myself off until you need me again" instead of just waiting at AUTONOMOUS_COMPLETE. Also the 20-tick ceiling feels generous; most tasks today completed in 2-6 ticks so either we're wasting compute or there's value in having that buffer for longer operations.

AUTONOMOUS_COMPLETE
